After reviewing the provided evidence, particularly item [1], which explicitly states that necrotic areas appear pale ("pale") and do not stain with TTC, whereas healthy tissue stains brick red — I confirm that the infarcted (necrotic) myocardium will remain unstained and thus appear white or pale. Final answer: \boxed{A} Based on the retrieved evidence, necrotic myocardium fails to take up triphenyltetrazolium chloride due to lack of mitochondrial metabolism, resulting in no color development and appearing pale or white. This aligns with established histopathological principles where viable tissue turns red upon TTC exposure while infarcted regions remain unstained.
